reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove G has a NORMAL Sylow subgroup for p, q, or r. The corrected version faithfully captures this as `(∃ (P : Sylow p G), P.Normal) ∨ ...`. The original formalization instead asserts only `Nonempty (Sylow p G) ∨ Nonempty (Sylow q G) ∨ Nonempty (Sylow r G)`, omitting the normality condition. Verified in Mathlib (Mathlib/GroupTheory/Sylow.lean line 177): `instance nonempty : Nonempty (Sylow p G)` is an unconditional instance, so `Nonempty (Sylow p G)` is always true. Thus the original's conclusion is a trivial tautology — strictly weaker than the informal claim. The corrected version implies the original, but not vice versa. The error is a misreading of the informal statement, dropping the word "normal".
